scq.question:
The IUPAC name of CH₃CH₂OH is:

(A) Methanol
(B) Ethanol
(C) Propanol
(D) Butanol
scq.solution: CH₃CH₂OH has 2 carbons with an -OH group, hence ethanol.

mcq.question:
Predict: The shape of SF₆ molecule is:

(A) Tetrahedral
(B) Square planar
(C) Octahedral
(D) Trigonal bipyramidal
mcq.solution: SF₆ has 6 bond pairs and 0 lone pairs — octahedral.

scq.question:
The equation of a circle with center (0,0) and radius 5 is:

(A) x² + y² = 5
(B) x² + y² = 25
(C) x + y = 5
(D) x² - y² = 25

mcq.question:
For a body in simple harmonic motion, which statements are correct?

(A) Velocity is maximum at mean position
(B) Acceleration is maximum at extreme position
(C) KE is maximum at extreme position
(D) Total energy is constant
mcq.solution: In SHM: v is max at mean, a is max at extreme, KE is max at mean (not extreme), total energy is constant.

mcq.question:
In a reaction, The VSEPR shape of NH₃ is:

(A) Tetrahedral
(B) Trigonal planar
(C) Trigonal pyramidal
(D) Linear
mcq.solution: NH₃ has 3 bp + 1 lp = trigonal pyramidal.

scq.question:
The derivative of x³ + 2x is:

(A) 3x² + 2
(B) x² + 2
(C) 3x² + 2x
(D) 3x + 2

Predict: The number of isomers of C₄H₁₀ is:

(A) 1
(B) 2
(C) 3
(D) 4

∫ 1/x dx equals:

(A) ln|x| + C
(B) x + C
(C) 1/x² + C
(D) -1/x² + C

A body cools from 80°C to 60°C in 5 min. By Newton's law, it cools to ~48°C in next 5 min if surrounding is at 20°C. The average temperature used is:

(A) 70°C and 54°C
(B) 80°C and 60°C
(C) 60°C and 48°C
(D) 50°C and 40°C
scq.solution: Newton's law: dT/dt ∝ (T_avg - T_surr). First interval avg = 70, second = ~54.

scq.question:
If f(x) = eˣ, then f'(x) is:

(A) eˣ

(C) 1/eˣ
(D) ln(x)

(A) -4 D
(B) +4 D

(D) +25 D

(A) 100%
(B) 50%
(C) 25%
(D) 75%

The catalyst used in Haber's process is:

(A) V₂O₅
(B) Pt
(C) Fe
(D) Ni
scq.solution: Iron (Fe) is the catalyst in Haber's process for NH₃ synthesis.

scq.question:
The catalyst used in Haber's process is:

(A) V₂O₅
(B) Pt
(C) Fe
(D) Ni
scq.solution: Iron (Fe) is the catalyst in Haber's process for NH₃ synthesis.

scq.question:
A wire of resistance R is stretched to double its length. New resistance is:

(A) R
(B) 2R
(C) 4R
(D) R/2
scq.solution: Volume constant: A' = A/2, l' = 2l. R' = ρ(2l)/(A/2) = 4R

scq.question:
The hybridization of carbon in diamond is:

(A) sp
(B) sp²
(C) sp³
(D) sp³d
scq.solution: Each carbon in diamond forms 4 sigma bonds — sp³.

scq.question:
Two identical springs of spring constant k are connected in series. Effective k is:

(A) 2k
(B) k
(C) k/2
(D) k/4

mcq.question:
Which of the following functions are continuous at x = 0?

(A) sin(x)/x (defined as 1 at 0)
(B) |x|
(C) 1/x
(D) eˣ
mcq.solution: sin(x)/x (with f(0)=1), |x|, and eˣ are continuous at 0. 1/x is not defined at 0.

scq.question:
The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about a diameter is:

(A) 2MR²/5
(B) MR²/2
(C) 2MR²/3
(D) MR²
scq.solution: Standard result: I = 2MR²/5 for solid sphere about diameter.

integerQuestion.question: The total number of fundamental forces in nature is ___

scq.question:
The hybridization of carbon in diamond is:

(A) sp
(B) sp²
(C) sp³
(D) sp³d
scq.solution: Each carbon in diamond forms 4 sigma bonds — sp³.

scq.question:
The work done by centripetal force on a body moving in a circle is:

(A) Positive
(B) Negative
(C) Zero
(D) Depends on speed
scq.solution: Centripetal force is perpendicular to velocity, so W = 0.

integerQuestion.question: A projectile is launched at 45° with speed 20 m/s. The maximum height reached in metres is ___ (g = 10 m/s²)

scq.question:
The general solution of dy/dx = y is:

(A) y = eˣ + C
(B) y = Ceˣ
(C) y = x + C
(D) y = Ce⁻ˣ
scq.solution: dy/y = dx. ln|y| = x + c. y = Ceˣ

scq.question:
The critical angle for total internal reflection depends on:

(A) Angle of incidence
(B) Refractive index
(C) Wavelength only
(D) Intensity
scq.solution: sin(c) = 1/μ. Critical angle depends on refractive index.

mcq.question:
The hybridization of carbon in CH₄ is:

(A) sp
(B) sp²
(C) sp³
(D) sp³d
mcq.solution: CH₄ has 4 bond pairs, so sp³ hybridization.

scq.question:
Which of the following has the highest ionization energy?

(A) Na
(B) Mg
(C) Al
(D) Ne
scq.solution: Noble gases have the highest ionization energy. Ne > Mg > Na > Al (approximately).

integerQuestion.solution: O=C=O has 2 π bonds (one in each C=O).

(D) 40 m

The value of lim(x→0) sin(x)/x is:

(A) 0
(B) 1

(D) -1

An ideal gas undergoes isothermal expansion. Its internal energy:

(A) Increases
(B) Decreases
(C) Remains constant
(D) First increases then decreases
scq.solution: For an ideal gas, internal energy depends only on temperature. In isothermal process, T is constant so ΔU = 0.

scq.question:

(D) Gauss
scq.solution: Magnetic flux Φ is measured in Weber (Wb).

scq.question:
Electronegativity increases:

(A) Down a group
(B) Left to right in a period
(C) With atomic size
(D) With metallic character
scq.solution: Electronegativity increases left to right across a period.

scq.question:
In an electrochemical cell, oxidation occurs at the:

(A) Cathode
(B) Anode
(C) Both
(D) Neither
scq.solution: Oxidation occurs at the anode (AN OX — anode oxidation).

scq.question:
Le Chatelier's principle applies to:

(A) Only exothermic reactions
(B) Only gaseous reactions
(C) Equilibrium systems
(D) Irreversible reactions
scq.solution: Le Chatelier's principle applies to systems at equilibrium.

mcq.solution: XeF₄ has 4 bp + 2 lp = square planar.

mcq.solution: β = λD/d. If d → d/2, β → 2β. Fringe width doubles.

(C) ½kx²
(D) 2kx²

mcq.question:
The normality of 1M H₂SO₄ is:

(A) 1 N
(B) 2 N
(C) 0.5 N
(D) 4 N
mcq.solution: N = M × basicity = 1 × 2 = 2 N

scq.question:

(A) 1
(B) 2
(C) 3
(D) 4

For a system where A simple pendulum has time period T. If its length is quadrupled, new time period is:

(A) T
(B) 2T
(C) 4T
(D) T/2

The drift velocity of electrons in a conductor is proportional to:

(A) Electric field
(B) Square of electric field
(C) Cube of current
(D) Temperature
scq.solution: vd = eEτ/m. Drift velocity is proportional to E.

integerQuestion.question: If two resistors of 6Ω and 3Ω are connected in parallel, the equivalent resistance in Ω is ___
The number of nodes in 3p orbital is ___
integerQuestion.solution: Total nodes = n-1 = 2. Angular nodes = l = 1. Radial nodes = 1. Total = 2.

integerQuestion.question: How many electrons are in Na⁺ (Z=11)? ___

scq.question:
Predict: Which of the following has the highest ionization energy?

(A) Na
(B) Mg
(C) Al
(D) Ne
scq.solution: Noble gases have the highest ionization energy. Ne > Mg > Na > Al (approximately).

(A) Tetrahedral
(B) Trigonal planar
(C) Trigonal pyramidal
(D) Linear

mcq.question:
Identify: Which of the following is a Lewis acid?

(A) NH₃
(B) BF₃
(C) H₂O
(D) NaOH
mcq.solution: BF₃ is electron deficient and accepts electron pairs — Lewis acid.

The order of reaction A → B is determined by:

(A) Stoichiometry
(B) Molecularity
(C) Experiment
(D) Temperature
scq.solution: Order of reaction is always determined experimentally.

An object is placed at the center of curvature of a concave mirror. Image is at:

(A) Focus
(B) Centre of curvature
(C) Infinity
(D) Between F and C
scq.solution: Object at C gives image at C, same size, real and inverted.

scq.question:
Which of the following is an electrophile?

(A) NH₃
(B) AlCl₃
(C) OH⁻
(D) CN⁻
scq.solution: AlCl₃ is electron deficient — it is an electrophile.

scq.solution: Magnetic flux Φ is measured in Weber (Wb).

scq.question:

(A) Reduction

(D) Roasting
scq.solution: Calcination converts carbonate ore to oxide by heating.

mcq.question:
Which of the following are conservative forces?

(A) Gravitational force
(B) Friction
(C) Electrostatic force
(D) Air resistance
mcq.solution: Gravitational and electrostatic forces are conservative. Friction and air resistance are non-conservative.

scq.solution: v = fλ. If f → 2f and v constant, λ → λ/2.

scq.solution: SF₆ has 6 bond pairs and 0 lone pairs — octahedral.

mcq.solution: Displacement and torque are vectors. Speed and temperature are scalars.

(A) 70°C and 54°C
(B) 80°C and 60°C
(C) 60°C and 48°C
(D) 50°C and 40°C
scq.solution: Newton's law: dT/dt ∝ (T_avg - T_surr). First interval avg = 70, second = ~54.

scq.solution: Hund's rule states that degenerate orbitals are filled singly first with parallel spins.
